headline: Recall of Medline Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter 10CC Syringe in Aligned Medical AMS6908E and AMS6908F Angio Packs Due to Risk of Disconnection

image.caption: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions Issues Nationwide Recall of Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter 10CC Control Syringe Placed into the Following Kits; AMS6908E Angio Pack, and AMS6908F Angio Pack

description: Windstone Medical Packaging recalls Aligned Medical Angio Packs AMS6908E and AMS6908F for syringe adapter risk causing biohazard exposure and air embolism.
keywords: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions

Risk of the syringe rotating adapter unwinding during use, which may result in a loose connection and/or full disconnection between the syringes and manifold, potentially causing biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Aligned Medical Solutions, operating as Windstone Medical Packaging, issued a nationwide recall of two Angio Pack convenience kits (AMS6908E and AMS6908F) containing the Medline Namic Angiographic Rotating Adapter (RA) 10CC Syringe. The recall is due to risk of the syringe rotating adapter unwinding during use, which can cause loose connection or full disconnection between syringes and manifold, potentially leading to biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

The recalled kits were manufactured between October 18, 2024, and November 13, 2025, and distributed from October 28, 2024, through November 28, 2025. Medline reported three incidents of inadvertent air injection and one biohazard exposure related to this issue, with no deaths reported. All units have the potential to exhibit this failure mode.

Only the Medline 10CC Control Syringe within these kits is recalled; other components are not affected. Replacement syringes and labeling are available through Aligned Medical Solutions. This recall is based on the company announcement and FDA posting.

Kit Name Product Code DI Number Lot Numbers Manufacture Dates
AMS6908E Angio Pack AMS6908E B098AMS6908E0 214875 10/18/2024 – 11/13/2025
AMS6908F Angio Pack AMS6908F B098AMS6908F0 215436, 225001, 226706, 234088 10/18/2024 – 11/13/2025

facts.company: Windstone Medical Packaging dba Aligned Medical Solutions
facts.reason: Risk of the syringe rotating adapter unwinding during use, which may result in a loose connection and/or full disconnection between the syringes and manifold, potentially causing biohazard exposure, blood loss, infection, or air embolism.

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers should check if their AMS6908E or AMS6908F Angio Packs contain the recalled Medline 10CC Control Syringe, stop using affected syringes, and contact Windstone Medical Packaging at 1-800-360-9428 or email fieldcorrectiveaction@alignedmedicalsolutions.com for replacement syringes and additional support.

content.subtitle: Company Contact Information
content.text: Consumer support: 1-800-360-9428 | fieldcorrectiveaction@alignedmedicalsolutions.com

content.subtitle: Source and Verification
content.text: According to the U.S. Food and Drug Administration (FDA), this recall notice was published on April 20, 2026.
authorityLinks: Read the official FDA recall notice on the FDA website.
Review consumer support information on the company website.
Visit the company website for product details.

headline: Revival Animal Health Recalls Breeder’s Edge and Shelter’s Choice Canine Milk Replacers Due to Variable Vitamin D Concentrations

image.caption: Revival Animal Health, LLC Voluntarily Recalls Canine Milk Replacers Due to Elevated Levels of Vitamin D

description: Revival Animal Health recalls Breeder’s Edge and Shelter’s Choice canine milk replacers due to variable vitamin D levels affecting dog health nationwide.

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Revival Animal Health of Orange City, IA is voluntarily recalling Breeder’s Edge® Foster Care® and Shelter’s Choice® Canine Milk Replacers due to variable levels of Vitamin D. Some products may contain either low or elevated Vitamin D, which can cause health issues in dogs. Elevated Vitamin D intake may lead to symptoms including vomiting, loss of appetite, increased thirst and urination, excessive drooling, and weight loss, with severe cases potentially causing renal dysfunction.

The recall affects multiple package sizes and best-by dates of canine milk replacers sold nationwide online and in pet stores. Two complaints of rickets have been reported, prompting this action after confirmation of inconsistent Vitamin D levels. The recall is conducted in coordination with the U.S. Food and Drug Administration.

Product NameNet WeightUPCBest By Dates
Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacer1 lb.8171600110876/19/2026, 9/9/2026, 1/16/2027, 11/14/2027
Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacer3.5 lb.8171600110706/19/2026, 10/31/2026, 11/14/2027, 1/20/2028
Shelter's Choice Canine Milk Replacer8 lb.8171600110566/19/2026, 9/9/2026, 11/14/2027, 1/20/2028
Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine Milk Replacer12 oz8171600101897/11/2026, 8/15/2026, 9/9/2026, 4/3/2027, 5/1/2027, 2/5/2028
Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine Milk Replacer18 lb.8171600110257/11/2026, 9/9/2026, 1/16/2027, 4/3/2027, 4/29/2027, 11/14/2027, 1/20/2028
Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine Milk Replacer20 lb.8171600101726/19/2026
Breeder's Edge Foster Care Canine Milk Replacer4.5 lb.8171600100735/22/2026, 9/9/2026, 10/31/2026, 3/7/2027, 4/29/2027, 11/14/2027, 2/5/2028

content.subtitle: What You Should Do
content.text: Consumers who have purchased any affected Breeder’s Edge or Shelter’s Choice canine milk replacer should check their product lot against the recall list and contact Revival Animal Health at 1-877-870-4059 for information on returning the product and obtaining a refund.

headline: Silver Moon LP DBA Loard’s Ice Cream Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk, Eggs, Tree Nuts, Peanuts, Soy, Wheat, Sulfites, and Food Color Additives in Ice Cream Products

image.caption: Silver Moon LP DBA Loard’s Ice Cream Issues Allergy Alert on Undeclared Milk, Eggs, Tree Nuts, Peanuts, Soy, Wheat, Sulfites, and Food Color Additives in Ice Cream Products

description: Loard’s announced a recall involving Ice Cream after issues linked to undeclared milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, sulfites, and colors. FDA recall...
keywords: Silver Moon LP dba Loard’s Ice Cream
Food & Beverages Ice Cream/Frozen Dairy Allergens
Undeclared Milk, Eggs, Tree Nuts, Peanuts, Soy, Sulfites, and Colors

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Silver Moon LP, doing business as Loard’s Ice Cream, of San Leandro, CA, is voluntarily recalling all retail-sized Loard’s Ice Cream products due to undeclared allergens, sulfites, and food color additives on the packaging. These products lack required ingredient labeling, posing a risk of serious allergic reactions to individuals sensitive to milk, eggs, tree nuts, peanuts, soy, or wheat.

The recalled ice cream was sold in Loard’s Ice Cream parlors in Northern California in 32oz blue paper cups and 56oz plastic cups. No illnesses related to this issue have been reported. The recall follows FDA inspection findings of missing allergen and ingredient information on retail packaging.

Consumers should be aware of the allergen risks associated with these products and check packaging to confirm if items are affected. Detailed information on flavors, undeclared allergens, colors, sulfites, and package sizes is provided below.

headline: Liquid Blenz Corp Recalls Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz Amber Bottles Over Potential Botulism Contamination

image.caption: Liquid Blenz Corp. Recalls Product Due to Possible Health Risk

description: Liquid Blenz Corp recalls Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz amber bottles due to potential botulism contamination. No illnesses reported to date.
keywords: Food & Beverages Carbonated Soft Drinks Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Liquid Blenz Corp of Rockville Center, NY, is recalling all lots of Good Brain Tonic due to a potential risk of botulism, a serious foodborne illness. Botulism can cause symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Immediate medical attention is recommended if symptoms occur.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide through retail stores and online. This recall includes Good Brain Tonic bottled in 16 oz and 32 oz Amber bottles with plastic caps. The botulism risk was identified through testing by the Cornell Food Venture Center and New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets.

No illnesses have been reported to date.

Product Size UPC Code
Good Brain Tonic 16 oz Amber bottle 860010984468
Good Brain Tonic 32 oz Amber bottle 860010984475

en.title: Liquid Blenz Corp Recalls Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz Due to Botulism Risk
en.headline: Liquid Blenz Corp Recalls Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz Amber Bottles Over Potential Botulism Contamination
en.description: Liquid Blenz Corp recalls Good Brain Tonic 16 oz and 32 oz amber bottles due to potential botulism contamination. No illnesses reported to date.
en.productType: Food & Beverages Carbonated Soft Drinks Foodborne Illness
en.disclaimer: When a company announces a recall, market withdrawal, or safety alert, the FDA posts the company's announcement as a public service. FDA does not endorse either the product or the company.
en.regulatedProducts: Food & Beverages Carbonated Soft Drinks Foodborne Illness, Food & Beverages, Carbonated Soft Drinks, Foodborne Illness

content.subtitle: Recall Summary
content.text:
Liquid Blenz Corp of Rockville Center, NY, is recalling all lots of Good Brain Tonic due to a potential risk of botulism, a serious foodborne illness. Botulism can cause symptoms such as general weakness, dizziness, double vision, difficulty speaking or swallowing, breathing problems, muscle weakness, abdominal distension, and constipation. Immediate medical attention is recommended if symptoms occur.

The recalled products were distributed nationwide through retail stores and online. This recall includes Good Brain Tonic bottled in 16 oz and 32 oz Amber bottles with plastic caps. The botulism risk was identified through testing by the Cornell Food Venture Center and New York State Department of Agriculture and Markets.

No illnesses have been reported to date.
